user_prompt: inside `lib\bluedroid\bluedroid_sbc.c` and `lib\bluedroid\bluedroid_sbc.h`, implement something similar to what is available from `lib\sbc\sbc.h`. It doesn't have to be identical, but make it easy to use with `lib\AudioManager`
agent_reply: - Added a usable C API in [bluedroid_sbc.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/bluedroid/bluedroid_sbc.h:42) and [bluedroid_sbc.c](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/bluedroid/bluedroid_sbc.c:174).
- API mirrors the useful parts of `lib/sbc/sbc.h`, but uses `bluedroid_sbc_*` names so both backends can coexist.
- Defaults to HFP mSBC settings: 16 kHz mono, 15 blocks, 8 subbands, bitpool 26.
- Updated [library.json](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/bluedroid/library.json) so `bluedroid_sbc.c` is included in the library build.
- Direct-compiled `lib\bluedroid\bluedroid_sbc.c` with the ESP32 Xtensa compiler successfully.
- `pio pkg pack lib\bluedroid` succeeds, though PlatformIO still prints that local `.platformio\.cache` ownership warning at shutdown.
- Full `pio run` still stops at the existing unrelated `Network.h` WiFi include-path issue before Bluedroid is pulled in.

user_prompt: I made changes in `lib\AudioManager` related to the new switch `USE_BLUEDROID_MSBC` which switches between using bluedroid and libsbc
review these changes, implement the marked TODO items
agent_reply: - `AudioManager` now includes [conf.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/conf.h) so `USE_BLUEDROID_MSBC` is actually visible there.
- Added `-I lib/bluedroid` to [platformio.ini](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini:12), since `AudioManager` includes `bluedroid_sbc.h` directly.
Review note: I didn’t find other functional issues in the switch code after the TODOs were filled. The one practical integration issue was the missing wrapper include path, which is now fixed.
- `rg` confirms the marked Bluedroid TODOs are gone.
- `lib\bluedroid\bluedroid_sbc.c` still compiles directly with the ESP32 Xtensa compiler.
- `pio pkg pack lib\bluedroid` succeeds, though PlatformIO still prints its local `.platformio\.cache` ownership warning.
- Full `pio run` still fails on the existing ESP32 WiFi `Network.h` include problem before a complete firmware link.

user_prompt: C:/Users/frank/.platformio/packages/toolchain-xtensa-esp-elf/bin/../lib/gcc/xtensa-esp-elf/13.2.0/../../../../xtensa-esp-elf/bin/ld.exe: .pio/build/theflybringuptest/firmware.elf section `.iram0.text' will not fit in region `iram0_0_seg'
C:/Users/frank/.platformio/packages/toolchain-xtensa-esp-elf/bin/../lib/gcc/xtensa-esp-elf/13.2.0/../../../../xtensa-esp-elf/bin/ld.exe: IRAM0 segment data does not fit.
collect2.exe: error: ld returned 1 exit status
ouch, we haven't even implemented half of the project yet
agent_reply: The mSBC undefined symbol is gone by changing [bluedroid_sbc.c](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/bluedroid/bluedroid_sbc.c) to use `OI_CODEC_SBC_DecoderReset(...)` and then set `mSBCEnabled = TRUE`, instead of calling the missing `OI_CODEC_mSBC_DecoderReset`.

The IRAM overflow was the ESP32 classic BT plus PSRAM combo. I disabled `BOARD_HAS_PSRAM` only for `[env:theflybringuptest]` in [platformio.ini](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini), which frees enough IRAM for this bringup test without changing the base env.
Also finished the previous cleanup: `print_bda` is gone from the BT tests; they use `log_bda`.

user_prompt: if our copy inside `lib\bluedroid\encoder` and `lib\bluedroid\decoder`, then block our own copy and use the included one from esp-idf
agent_reply: Done. I blocked our local `lib/bluedroid/encoder` and `lib/bluedroid/decoder` sources from compiling and switched the wrapper to use ESP-IDF’s included Bluedroid SBC symbols instead.
- `platformio.ini` now exposes the ESP-IDF BT/SBC include paths
- Removed the earlier local symbol-prefix workaround

I also fixed the wrapper for the ESP-IDF encoder shape, including copying PCM into `as16PcmBuffer` when `SBC_NO_PCM_CPY_OPTION` is false. That is likely the actual crash fix for the `EncPacking` StoreProhibited path.
- The map file now shows SBC encoder/decoder symbols coming from ESP-IDF `libbt.a`, while our library only contributes `bluedroid_sbc.c` as the adapter.

- The map file now shows SBC encoder/decoder symbols coming from ESP-IDF `libbt.a`, while our library only contributes `bluedroid_sbc.c` as the adapter.
